game_info.title: Stickman Golf - Physics Challenge
game_info.concept: **Super Stickman Golf 2:** Players control a stickman golfer, dragging a finger on the screen to adjust shot angle and power, then releasing to hit the golf ball across 2D physics-based courses. The objective is to sink the ball into the hole in the fewest strokes possible, navigating dynamic obstacles like moving platforms, portals, and magnets. Each shot consumes one stroke, and players can activate limited-use power-ups (e.g., 3 sticky balls per round) before a shot to alter ball physics. Progression involves unlocking 32 new courses by completing previous ones, and earning "Golf Bux" from low stroke counts or collecting "Golf Balls" on courses. This currency unlocks 14 new golfer skins and 34 hats, with each hat providing a unique passive ability (e.g., increased shot accuracy or bounce). Multiplayer modes include real-time "Race Mode" for up to 3 online players to reach the hole first, and asynchronous "Turn Based Multiplayer" where players take turns on a course.
game_info.description: Navigate your stickman golfer through challenging physics-based courses to sink the ball into the hole in the fewest strokes possible. Aim carefully and adjust your power to overcome obstacles like moving platforms, bouncy surfaces, and hazards. Complete courses to unlock new levels and earn currency to purchase power-ups. Each stroke counts toward your score—master the physics to achieve par or better!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (Left/Right): Adjust shot angle
Arrow Keys (Up/Down): Adjust shot power
Space: Take the shot
S: Activate Sticky Ball power-up (limited uses)
W: Activate Power Boost power-up (limited uses)
Enter: Start game from START screen
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (from GAME_OVER screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game mechanics: ball physics, stroke counting, and hole completion. This ensures the core gameplay loop works correctly with proper collision detection and win condition triggering.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically aim toward the hole and take shots with moderate power. The test will make 3-5 shots attempting to reach the hole, adjusting angle slightly between shots to navigate around obstacles.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The ball should move realistically with physics, strokes should increment with each shot, and the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN when the ball enters the hole. Test is successful if win condition triggers within 10 strokes.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing power-up system and multiple course progression. Verifies that sticky ball and power boost power-ups work correctly and that completing a course unlocks the next one.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use power-ups strategically during shots—activate sticky ball for difficult landings and power boost for long distances. Complete the first course efficiently (under 5 strokes) to unlock the next course.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Power-ups should modify ball behavior visibly (sticky reduces bounce, boost increases power). Course completion should unlock the next level, and power-up counters should decrease with each use. Success means completing at least one course with power-ups.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing obstacle interactions including moving platforms, bouncy surfaces, and hazards. Ensures all interactive elements behave correctly and affect ball movement appropriately.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally aim shots to interact with each type of obstacle—hit moving platforms to test timing, bounce off walls, and trigger hazards to test reset mechanics.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Moving platforms should carry the ball, bouncy surfaces should increase velocity, and hazards should reset the ball to the starting position. All obstacle types should interact predictably with the ball physics.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions: shooting at extreme angles, maximum power shots, and attempting shots while ball is still moving.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Execute shots at 0° and 180° angles, use maximum and minimum power levels, and attempt to queue multiple shots rapidly to test input buffering and state management.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Extreme angles should work without errors, power levels should be clamped appropriately, and the game should prevent new shots while the ball is moving. No crashes or physics glitches should occur.
TEST_4.description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions: shooting at extreme angles, maximum power shots, and attempting shots while ball is still moving.

TEST_4.expected: Extreme angles should work without errors, power levels should be clamped appropriately, and the game should prevent new shots while the ball is moving. No crashes or physics glitches should occur.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing game state transitions and phase management. Verifies that all game phases (START, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER) transition correctly and maintain proper state.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Cycle through all game phases: start game, pause mid-shot, unpause, complete course, restart, and repeat. Test phase transitions at different points in gameplay.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: All phase transitions should work smoothly without state corruption. Pausing should freeze gameplay, unpausing should resume correctly, and restart should reset all game state to initial values. gameState.gamePhase should always reflect the current phase accurately.
TEST_5.description: Testing game state transitions and phase management. Verifies that all game phases (START, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER) transition correctly and maintain proper state.
TEST_5.strategy: Cycle through all game phases: start game, pause mid-shot, unpause, complete course, restart, and repeat. Test phase transitions at different points in gameplay.
TEST_5.expected: All phase transitions should work smoothly without state corruption. Pausing should freeze gameplay, unpausing should resume correctly, and restart should reset all game state to initial values. gameState.gamePhase should always reflect the current phase accurately.
